title: Mutual tail calls are not TCO'd: ping-pong recursion crashes at ~300-500k depth
goal: Found by the P4 stress harness (2026-07-02, master 2692ca63). Self tail recursion is optimized (10^8 iterations O(1) stack) but MUTUAL tail calls burn a frame per hop: (define (ping n) (if (= n 0) 'ping (pong (- n 1)))) with reciprocal pong crashes SIGILL at depth 500000 (300000 passes, no diagnostic); 10^7 dies at ~535MB RSS. JIT and AOT identical. R7RS 3.5 requires proper tail calls across function boundaries. Likely needs musttail (or the trampoline the self-TCO path uses) for calls in tail position to other known functions.
acceptance: tests/stress/found/mutual_tail_1e7.esk prints OK ping in O(1) stack under -r and AOT
rec_mutual.esk mutual-tail depth can be raised to 10^7
campaign_payoff: State machines written as mutually tail-calling functions (the canonical Scheme idiom) currently have a hidden ~300k transition ceiling.

verifications.evidence: mutual_tail_1e7.esk still SIGILLs (rc 132): mutual tail calls not TCO'd. Stress XKNOWN.

Non-self calls in tail position now emit LLVM `musttail` (llvm_codegen.cpp codegenCall mutual-TCO block) instead of the old TCK_Tail hint (which the backend ignored). Guarded by matching signature/calling-convention and no pointer-into-frame args (the latter is what made X86 reject closure-forwarding stdlib wrappers). mutual_tail_1e7.esk now prints 'OK ping' in O(1) stack on BOTH -r (0.87s, 225MB) and AOT (0.47s, 27MB). P6b recursion_depth sweep: mutual_tail2/mutual_tail3 PASS to 5,000,000 hops on both lanes; even?/odd? 10M ok. stdlib builds clean on arm64. Limitation: higher-order tail calls that forward a stack-allocated closure argument still fall back to a bounded call (musttail is illegal with frame-pointer args).
